subject: Mathematics
title: Similar Triangles
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Similar Triangles.

hook.text: Similar triangles have the same shape but different sizes—like a photo and its enlargement.

buildup.text: Their corresponding angles are equal, and their sides are in proportion.

discovery.text: Thales used similar triangles to measure the height of the Great Pyramid using only its shadow.

twist.text: Every right triangle with the same angles is similar. This is why trigonometry works at any scale.

climax.text: Architects use similar triangles to scale blueprints into real buildings.

punchline.text: Same shape, different size—and the ratios never lie.

quiz.question: How did Thales measure the Great Pyramid's height?
options: Using similar triangles and shadow lengths
Climbing to the top with a rope
